docs.title: Catch Invisible Breaks in Time Series
docs.text: TSI Analytics is a next-generation geometric framework that instantly detects structural shifts, regime changes, and crisis moments that classical correlation methods completely miss.

docs.title: Why TSI?
docs.text:
Traditional methods focus only on the "magnitude" (amplitude) of data, while TSI reads the data as a geometric trajectory. Even if two systems produce similar values, they can diverge behaviorally. We measure this directional consistency.

!!! success "Core Advantages of TSI"

* **Speed & Scalability:** Operates in real-time at $O(n)$ complexity without bottlenecking your systems, unlike heavy Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) algorithms.

* **Natural Noise Filter:** Mathematically minimizes noise variance by 4x using a secant-based geometric architecture, without applying destructive low-pass filters to your raw data.

* **Early Warning System (Event Horizon):** Predicts the exact moment a single signal will change regimes before the crash happens, without needing an external reference signal.

docs.title: Proven Cross-Domain Performance
docs.text:
Our proprietary framework has been rigorously tested and validated across multiple industries: * Financial Algorithms: Provided a 28.4% higher capital preservation and return advantage over standard "HODL" strategies during crypto bear markets.

  • Biomedical Signals: Maintained structural integrity on raw, uncalibrated EEG datasets with only a 3.8% performance degradation compared to heavily preprocessed data.

  • Industrial IoT & Radar: Reduced false positive motion alarms by 98% in noisy Wi-Fi CSI environments and achieved 99.1% authentication accuracy under severe Doppler shifts.

Explore Our Technology ->{ .md-button .md-button--primary }

docs.title: Our Technology: Multiscale Geometric Analysis
docs.text:
The TSI architecture examines time series through the lens of computational geometry rather than classical numerical analysis.

While the exact mathematical weighting and Laplace kernel formulations remain our commercial IP, the fundamental logic of the system rests on three main pillars:

docs.title: 1. Event Horizon & Multiscale Intersection Area (MIA)
docs.text:
When a system enters a crisis or a structural break, our algorithm calculates hidden "Intersection Triangles" formed by the divergence of the system's own micro, meso, and macro trajectories. Millisecond spikes in this area alert us to structural stress long before the actual crisis erupts.

Figure 1: TSI's performance profile on noisy raw signals. The algorithm detects the breaking point in advance and warns that the system is about to change direction via the "Event Horizon" metric.

docs.title: 2. Structural Gap Metric (SGM)
docs.text:
Optimized specifically for IoT devices and High-Frequency Trading (HFT) bots, this module instantly collides the system's forward and backward trend predictions. The vertical "structural gap" between them is the clearest evidence that the system is about to change frequency or direction. It operates with zero trigonometric functions, making it ultra-lightweight.

Figure 2: The exact moment a synthetic wave changes frequency. The lower graph shows how the MIA module spikes instantly to warn the system of the structural disruption.

docs.title: 3. The Ultimate Decision Engine: UGSI (Unified Geometric Stability Index)
docs.text: For extreme noise environments and critical sensor networks, all our geometric modules (GSCI, MIA, SGM) merge under a single "Hyper-Metric". UGSI utilizes Bayesian weighting to analyze instantaneous jumps and structural bends simultaneously. This ensures flawless crisis detection reliability by reducing the false alarm rate to near zero.

docs.title: Enterprise Solutions & Integration
docs.text: We offer API access and B2B consulting services to integrate the TSI framework into your company's sensor networks, algorithmic trading bots, or predictive maintenance systems.

docs.title: Biomedical Signal and Health Data Analysis
docs.text: TSI overcomes patient-to-patient calibration issues in clinical data by focusing directly on structural patterns rather than amplitude.

docs.title: 1. Long-Term Disease Progression (Blood Tests)
docs.text:
The TSI algorithm perceives deteriorations in patients' long-term blood values (WBC, Glucose, ALT) as "Trend Stress" (MIA), independently of reference ranges.

Figure: Compared to healthy individuals; the dramatic spikes in the MIA (Disease Stress) area, shown in red, created by TSI during the development of Infection (WBC), Diabetes (Glucose), and Liver Disease (ALT).

docs.title: 2. Neurological Patterns (EEG Dataset)
docs.text:
Our algorithm operates independently of the signal quality from clinical devices.

Figure: TSI demonstrating a high structural matching score of 0.909 on average across raw and smooth EEG signals taken from the SEED-IV dataset.

docs.title: Defense Industry and Signal Processing
docs.text: In electronic warfare, radar systems, and aerospace communications, detecting and compensating for signal shifts caused by high-speed targets is a critical engineering problem.

docs.title: Doppler Shift Compensation
docs.text:
The TSI algorithm aligns phase and frequency mismatches between Transmitter and Receiver with a significantly lower processor load compared to classical Fourier transforms.

Figure: Compensation of Doppler shift in a signal received from a high-speed aircraft using TSI. The bottom-left graph shows how the algorithm captures the optimal match (Max TSI = 0.989) with a flawless peak at q = -14.50. The right side demonstrates the error margin dropping to zero after alignment.

docs.title: TSI Algorithm in Finance and Crypto Markets
docs.text: Catching regime shifts (Bull/Bear) is crucial for High-Frequency Trading (HFT) and algorithmic markets. TSI anticipates crises by focusing not on the direction of prices, but on the "geometric structure" of the market.

docs.title: Cryptocurrency Analysis: Foreseeing the Decoupling
docs.text:
Assets like Bitcoin and Ethereum, which normally move with high correlation, detach from each other during moments of structural crisis. TSI detects this decoupling much earlier than classical methods.

Figure: BTC and ETH price movements between 2023-2024. The middle graph shows how the TSI similarity score maintains structural integrity, while the bottom graph illustrates the concentration of the "Event Horizon" metric during crisis moments (zero point).

docs.title: Risk Management & Trading Bot Performance
docs.text:
Our hybrid trading algorithm, combining TSI's geometric regime detection with traditional indicators, delivers striking results: * Bear Market Advantage: Especially during the harsh market crash of 2022, it provided investors with a 28.4% capital preservation and return advantage compared to the standard "Buy and Hold" (HODL) strategy. TSI structurally recognizes downtrends and rapidly protects the portfolio.

Şekil: 2023-2024 yılları arasındaki BTC ve ETH fiyat hareketleri. Orta grafikte TSI benzerlik skorunun yapısal bütünlüğü nasıl koruduğu, alt grafikte ise "Event Horizon" metriğinin kriz (sıfır noktası) anlarındaki yoğunlaşması görülmektedir.

docs.title: Industrial IoT and Wi-Fi Sensor Networks
docs.text: Detecting anomalies in physical systems is a critical step for predictive maintenance.

docs.title: Precise Motion Detection via Wi-Fi Signals (CSI)
docs.text:
While traditional RSSI (signal strength) methods generate numerous false alarms, TSI's geometric analysis completely solves this issue.

Figure: While the classical RSSI method (top left) constantly detects false motion due to ambient noise, the TSI Method (top right) filters the noise using GSCI and SGM modules, flawlessly detecting ONLY the actual seconds of human motion.

docs.title: Ultra-Low Power Consumption (Edge Computing)
docs.text: SGM, the hardware-friendly module of TSI, requires zero trigonometric or complex calculations. Operating with just 6 basic arithmetic operations, it works flawlessly on FPGA-based embedded systems and ultra-low power (<1mW) wake-up radio applications.

docs.title: Industrial Physics, Thermodynamics, and Material Science
docs.text: TSI technology revolutionizes not only time-varying data but also physical processes where variables are interdependent (e.g., Pressure-Volume diagrams). Geometry is more universal than amplitude.

docs.title: 1. Early Detection of Phase Transitions
docs.text: In thermodynamic processes (such as liquid-gas phase transitions), classical derivative methods become useless due to noise or require manual thresholds. The TSI algorithm automatically captures the structural bend (using MIA and SGM) in P-V diagrams without needing any external reference or manual threshold.

docs.title: 2. Spectroscopic Material Fingerprinting
docs.text: Distinguishing materials in UV-stimulated spectrum analyses (400-700 nm) in noisy environments is a challenging problem. By tracking the geometric route of radiation curves, TSI classifies materials like Quartz, Diamond, or Sapphire with 96.3% accuracy even in highly noisy environments (SNR=10 dB). (Classical peak matching methods remain at an 84.1% accuracy rate).
